entities.name: AGRICULTURAL TECHNOLOGY ADOPTION
entities.description: The process of farmers deciding to use new agricultural technologies

entities.name: OUTPUT MARKET INTEGRATION
entities.description: The extent to which farmers participate in and sell their produce in the output market

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The country where the study was conducted

entities.name: DOUBLE-HURDLE MODEL
entities.description: A statistical model used to analyze the determinants of technology adoption intensity conditional on overcoming constraints

entities.name: SEED ACCESS CONSTRAINTS
entities.description: Barriers that prevent farmers from obtaining seeds

entities.name: TREATMENT EFFECT MODEL
entities.description: A model used to estimate the impact of technology adoption on market integration

entities.name: PROPENSITY SCORE REGRESSION
entities.description: A statistical technique used to account for heterogeneity in adoption decisions

entities.name: MATCHING TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Methods used to account for unobservable characteristics of farmers and their farms

entities.name: KNOWLEDGE OF EXISTING VARIETIES
entities.description: Farmers' awareness and information regarding available crop varieties

entities.name: PERCEPTION OF IMPROVED VARIETIES
entities.description: Farmers' views on the attributes and benefits of improved crop varieties

entities.name: HOUSEHOLD WEALTH
entities.description: Economic assets of the household, specifically livestock and land

entities.name: LABOR FORCE AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The availability of active labor to implement agricultural technologies

entities.name: IMPROVED AGRICULTURAL TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Advanced farming tools, seeds, or methods designed to increase productivity

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The efficiency of production, which increases with the adoption of improved technology
